introduction: This presentation will provide an overview of our HR onboarding activities in Q2. We will analyse how it impacts our overall operations and efficiency.

subsections.heading: New Hires Breakdown in Q2
subsections.content:
• A total of 250 new hires joined Elexion Automotive in Q2
• The Engineering department saw the highest influx of new hires, with 80 new employees
• The Sales and Marketing departments recruited 70 and 40 new members respectively
• R&D department had 30 new hires, while the remaining were scattered among other departments
• The average age of new hires was 28 years, with a 60-40 male to female ratio
• Majority of new hires were sourced from job portals and referrals
subsections.justification: This slide focuses on the demographics of new hires, not the number of HR onboarding sessions.

subsections.heading: Q2 HR Onboarding Sessions Analysis
subsections.content:
• The HR department introduced a new onboarding program in Q2
• The new onboarding program focused on integrating company culture and values
• It involved department heads as speakers to give an overview of their respective departments
• The feedback received for the new onboarding program was predominantly positive
• The program included a tour of the facility and an introduction to the company's mission and vision
• A mentorship initiative was also introduced as part of the onboarding program
subsections.justification: This slide discusses the nature and components of the onboarding sessions, not their quantity.

subsections.heading: Impact of Onboarding on Employee Efficiency
subsections.content:
• Employee efficiency showed an upward trend post onboarding
• The new onboarding program was found to decrease the time taken by new hires to adapt to their roles
• Surveys suggested that new hires felt more welcomed and prepared after the onboarding process
• A 15% improvement in productivity was observed in new hires after attending the onboarding sessions
• The well-structured onboarding program also helped to reduce the attrition rate among new hires
• The feedback from department heads indicated that the onboarding program helped new hires integrate faster
subsections.justification: This slide focuses on the outcomes of the onboarding sessions, not the number of sessions held.

conclusion: Understanding our onboarding sessions and their impact on efficiency gives us a better insight into our HR efforts. Going forward, we need to focus on improving these sessions for better productivity and employee satisfaction.

image_queries.slide_title: Impact of Onboarding on Employee Efficiency
